headline: China’s Victory Giant Seeks Up to $2.2 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. began taking investor orders for a listing in Hong Kong that may raise as much as HK$17.5 billion ($2.2 billion) in what is set to be among the city’s largest first-time share sales this year. The maker of printed circuit board products — the intricate electronic backbone of artificial-intelligence servers — is offering 83.3 million shares at as much as HK$209.88 each, according to a listing document released on Monday. headline: How The Liberty Global (LBTYA) Investment Story Is Shifting With Fresh Analyst Targets
summary: The latest research refresh on Liberty Global nudges fair value estimates slightly higher, with one model moving from US$15.84 to US$15.89 per share and other commentary pointing to targets around US$14. These fine tuning moves are being framed by analysts as adjustments to existing views rather than a reset of the story, with modest changes in targets tied to refreshed assumptions after recent results. As you read on, you will see how these numbers feed into the evolving analyst narrative...

headline: Morgan Stanley Says Earnings Shield S&P 500 From War
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Accelerating earnings are protecting the S&P 500 from deeper losses and masking a broader pullback in US equities, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley.The team led by Michael Wilson points to resilient earnings and continued economic recovery as reasons the S&P 500 has fallen less than 10% since hitting a record high in January. Beneath the surface, they see evidence that stocks are in the “final phase” of a correction.There are better gauges to assess the US stocks retreat

headline: Had You Invested $1,000 in Goldman Sachs 10 Years Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now
summary: Goldman Sachs (NYSE: GS) reported Q1 2026 EPS of $17.55 versus a FactSet estimate of $16.47, with revenue of $17.23 billion, up 14%, against a $16.99 billion estimate. Net earnings rose 18% to $5.63 billion, EPS climbed 24%, equity trading revenue rose 27%, and investment banking fees rose 48%. Yet the stock fell approximately 4% ... Had You Invested $1,000 in Goldman Sachs 10 Years Ago, Here’s What You’d Have Now

headline: Is S&P 500 correction over? Morgan Stanley leans bullish
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley is cautiously leaning bullish on U.S. equities, arguing that markets are further along in pricing out uncertainty than many investors appreciate, even as geopolitical risks and central bank inflation concerns keep sentiment fragile.

headline: BlackRock Pivots Back to US Stocks With Strategists Seeing Iran War ‘Contained’
summary: (Bloomberg) -- BlackRock strategists have returned to an overweight view on US stocks as they see the damage to global economic growth from the Middle East conflict “likely contained.”The firm had been watching “two signposts to dial up risk-taking” after shifting to neutral as the Middle East conflict led to reducing risk a few weeks ago, strategists led by Jean Boivin, the head of the BlackRock Investment Institute, wrote in a Monday note. Those signals were moves to restart shipping via the S
